title: pNetwork token mapping or configuration error historical bridge incident

historical_exploit.attack_summary: An address on the BNB Chain minted more than $1 billion of pGALA tokens out of thin air, and sold them through PancakeSwap to make a profit. The pGALA contract hacker has made a profit of $4.3 million. One Smart Money address arbitraged nearly $6.5 million in this attack, even more than the attacker's profit. Multi-link is tweeted by the protocol pNetwork, and the pGALA contract on the BNB Chain needs to be redeployed due to the misconfiguration of the cross-chain bridge. Huobi Global announced that it would re-list GALA after proposing that the GALA purchased after the abnormal event would be renamed pGALA, and the project party agreed to pay full compensation to the holders of the currency before the accident.
historical_exploit.attacker_profile: External attacker or unprivileged caller as described by the SlowMist incident row; exact actor permissions must be re-verified before reporting.
preconditions: A bridge, cross-chain router, gateway, wrapped-asset minter, liquidity pool, or validator/proof path accepts attacker-controlled message, proof, signature, transaction, deposit, configuration, or accounting input.
The accepted input releases existing bridge-held value, mints unbacked wrapped assets, miscredits a deposit, or lets a caller move assets beyond entitlement.
The target mapping must be reachable without relying on privileged key compromise, DNS/frontend phishing, social-account compromise, or pure rug-pull behavior.

value_path.asset_or_reward: bridge-held assets, wrapped assets, bridge liquidity, withdrawals, refunds, or backed cross-chain liabilities
value_path.how_value_was_extracted: An address on the BNB Chain minted more than $1 billion of pGALA tokens out of thin air, and sold them through PancakeSwap to make a profit. The pGALA contract hacker has made a profit of $4.3 million. One Smart Money address arbitraged nearly $6.5 million in this attack, even more than the attacker's profit. Multi-link is tweeted by the protocol pNetwork, and the pGALA contract on the BNB Chain needs to be redeployed due to the misconfiguration of the cross-chain bridge. Huobi Global announced that it would re-list GALA after proposing that the GALA purchased after the abnormal event would be renamed pGALA, and the project party agreed to pay full compensation to the holders of the currency before the accident.
value_path.why_it_paid: The historical row describes real loss, attempted bridge theft, unbacked minting, unauthorized withdrawal, or bridge-accounting failure that can be useful only if BridgeIndex has a matching current live-value extraction path.

mapping.why_this_might_map: BridgeIndex exposes bridge deposits, bot-gas accounting, withdrawal creation/approval/execution, refunds, token/resource mappings, relayer voting, and proposal execution. This historical primitive is useful only if it maps to extracting current BNB/USDT ledger value or backed bridge liabilities from the deployed BridgeIndex system.

notes: Selected for scanned because: bridge configuration error allowed unbacked pGALA mint
This seed is not proof that BridgeIndex is vulnerable; Workflow 7 must reject it unless the primitive maps to non-admin extraction of a current live ledger row.
